frameworks.calculating: Calculating scores for all three frameworks…

profile.heroImprovingTitle: Disclosure readiness is moving in the right direction
profile.heroImprovingBody: Renewable share improved by {{renewableDelta}} while Scope 1 emissions moved down by {{scope1Delta}}. This is the strongest near-term signal on the page.
profile.heroDivergenceBody: Current framework spread is {{spread}}, which suggests the company looks materially different depending on the regulatory lens.
profile.heroCoverageTitle: The company profile is becoming reusable analysis infrastructure
profile.heroCoverageBody: This case already combines {{periods}} reporting periods with {{frameworks}} framework views, making it useful for repeat analysis instead of one-off reading.
profile.radarLegend: Radar compares current overall score by framework
profile.trendLegend: Lines: Scope 1 (red), Renewable % (green), Taxonomy aligned revenue % (indigo)
profile.yoyScope1Hint: Negative values mean emissions decreased, which is usually positive.
profile.yoyTaxonomyHint: Tracks change in aligned revenue ratio where disclosed.
profile.yoyNarrativeReady: Compared with {{year}}, renewable share moved {{renewableDelta}} and taxonomy-aligned revenue moved {{taxonomyDelta}}. This is where the next version should explain the business or disclosure reason behind the change.
profile.yoyNarrativeMissing: There is not enough prior-period data yet to generate a strong year-over-year narrative.
profile.noFrameworkResults: No saved framework results yet. Run framework scoring first.
profile.whyIntro: This page is designed to prove that the product is more than a static ESG dashboard: it preserves time, framework, and evidence context in one reusable company view.
profile.whyPoint1Body: It shows that the product can translate public disclosures into multi-period, version-aware analysis instead of one-off extraction.
profile.whyPoint2Body: A client or analyst can see how readiness changes over time, not just what one report said in isolation.
profile.whyPoint3Body: The strongest long-term moat is not the chart itself, but the evidence-backed data structure and the ability to compare sources, periods, and frameworks.

manual.title: Manual Input / Case Builder
manual.subtitle: Build reliable demo companies even when API quota is gone. Save structured company-year records locally, keep period context, and reuse them across profile and framework pages.
manual.storageHint: This page writes to your local backend database. Same company + year currently updates the existing record.
manual.metricsHint: Fill only the fields you already trust. Missing values stay explicit and can be backfilled later.
manual.overwriteHint: Current storage is still keyed by company + year, so using the same pair will update the existing record. That is acceptable for annual baseline cases and temporary showcase drafts.
manual.heroBody: This is the practical bridge between portfolio design and real product development: we can keep building multi-year showcase cases without depending on model quota.
manual.heroPoint1Body: You can prepare interview-ready company stories faster by entering trusted values first and polishing automation later.
manual.heroPoint2Body: The same record can flow into company profiles, framework scoring, and future merge logic instead of living in scattered notes.
